check.command: awk -F: 'NR==FNR{uid[$1]=$3; shell[$1]=$7; next} ($1 in uid) && uid[$1]>=1000 && shell[$1] !~ /(nologin|false)$/ && $4 < 1 {print $1 " " $4}' /etc/passwd /etc/shadow

candidate_check.description: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux operating system must be configured so that passwords are restricted to a 24 hours/1 day minimum lifetime.

check_content_excerpt:
Check whether the minimum time period between password changes for each user account is one day or greater.

# awk -F: '$4 < 1 {print $1 " " $4}' /etc/shadow

If any results are returned that are not associated with a system account, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure non-compliant accounts to enforce a 24 hours/1 day minimum password lifetime:

# chage -m 1 [user]
title: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux operating system must be configured so that passwords are restricted to a 24 hours/1 day minimum lifetime.
